entry.title: Re: SAP ERP – PP61 T-Code – Managerial control for relate...
entry.content: Is there a different cloud solution that works integrated with SAP or an alternative standard tool in SAP SuccessFactors Platform or SAP Fiori for SAP ERP HCM that will meet this need (Shift Planning)?

entry.title: Re: Line Approval or rejection by Approving Authorities
entry.content:
Hi Nidhi,

The requirement is little bit different. While an approver is approving the document he should able to approve partial line items.

For Example, One Document is having 5 line items and approver wants to approve only 2 line items.

entry.title: Re: Messages in SAC Handling with BW Live Connection
entry.content: Thank you for your answer. Regarding the first idea, I haven't found any script so far that can visualize or retrieve the system messages. Only system-level traces can be retrieved, but these are system models like ERROR_LOG. As for the second idea of connecting to the BW system, I initially saw it as somewhat complicated to do, but I will look into it. Thanks!

entry.title: What is dump DBIF_NTAB_TABLE_NOT_FOUND?
entry.content:
Hi,

I've got a dump which is DBIF_NTAB_TABLE_NOT_FOUND. The error analysis is like below: Structure description "XXXX" was not found. In the event of a missing structure description for an include, this error may have occurred while accessing a structure or a table in which this include is used.

This error happened in an interface method calling. Does anyone explain more about this dump?

Regards,

Eric

entry.title: Re: How to display custom data in the custom section of f...
entry.content: I've created a section, and using odata v4, there is one large binary data in the api, that is not getting displayed in that section

entry.title: Re: How to display custom data in the custom section of f...
entry.content:
I've created a section, and using v4 service, there is one large binary data in the service, that is not getting displayed in that section by direct binding, it is showing path of that large binary data, now somehow i want to read that data and want to display it over section.

<core:FragmentDefinition
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:macros="sap.fe.macros"
    xmlns:code="sap.ui.codeeditor"
>
    <code:CodeEditor
        id="_IDGenCodeEditor1"
        core:require="{ handler: '.....'}"
        type="xml"
        value="{model>/data}"
        editable="false"
        height="100%"
        width="100%"
        maxLines="100000"
    />
</core:FragmentDefinition>

entry.title: Re: Basics of Nodejs and Expressjs in SAP BAS
entry.content: What does this article have to do with Business Application Studio or SAP? Moreover, it is not aligned with the SAP best practices of application development, which are outlined in the SAP BTP Developer's Guide linked here: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-guidance-framework/guidance-framework/what-is-sap-btp-guidance-framework

entry.title: Re: Purchasing unit not being respected when converting P...
entry.content:
If a conversion from PPDS is triggered then it is done with the old logic so with ME21 and not the enjoy version.
There is a difference. Have you checked if you convert the order in ECC /S4 with the old logic I guess you will get the same result in that as you perform the conversion from PPDS.

If this is the same result as via PPDS then I think there is a way to "switch" the the new conversion logic in PPDS during CIF to ECC and S4. See note 1737155 Using new me21n enjoy functionality for PO from APO.

entry.title: Changed tariff number
entry.content:
We have received a notification from Descartes that some tariff codes are changed. We have uploaded the xml file and tried to reclassify the products.

When we copy data, we get this message: " 7219310010 Consigned from Taiwan or Vietnam". The new code is uploaded in the system, but what that this message actually mean?

Note: seems like this is the Description of the code - Descartes confirmed this is correct.

entry.title: How to display landed cost of multiped PO
entry.content: How I can get the landed cost list against PO list. If I am not authorized for the SAP Tables. I want to know the standard T. Code for that if SAP provided.

entry.title: Re: How to get geolocation data through CDS view?
entry.content: Oh, thank you! I was confused because I was sure it would be possible if the annotations existed. Guess I was wrong. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: insufficient privilege currency conversion to shared...
entry.content:
Hi @XaviPolo,

I think his Problem is not creating or using the Currency Conversion. (Based on TCUR Tables from another space)
His Problem is SHARING that VIEW with Transcationaldata where the Currency Conversion took place to the next Layer.

e.G. 
Inbound Space - You create TCUR Tables from BW
HARMONISATION  - Nothing
PROGAGATION - NOW you are using the CC and it's working fine based on the TCUR Tables from Inbound Space 
REPORTING - HERE we have the Problem now you create e.g. a AM based on that PROGAGTION LAYER View and you recieve the Error.

We are having the same problem, one quick fix was to persist the result of the PROGAGTION LAYER View. (with this the Currency Conversion is done with "PROPGATION Layer User" and when you access the Data with the "REPORTING LAYER User" you don't have to do CC anymore).
So far we had no time deep diving this topic and try out some better Scenarios (e.g. creating own Wrapper View's instead of using the SAP one), but if someone has one I would be intrested 🙂 .

BR
Julian

entry.title: Role & responsibilities of MM consultant in computer manufacturing company like dell,HP,lenevo
entry.content:
Hi all,

Need your help to understand the roles and daily activities performed by MM consultant in a supply chain industry like dell or hp or any manufacturing industry?

I am new to SAP (learner) so i need some understanding the course related to the job role. Thanks for your help.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 HANA - Variant Configuration, Does it require...
entry.content:
Good morning Khaled!

Yes indeed, Advanced Variant Configuration has its own extra license. It is split into Professional (Modeler type of user) and Standard (Consumer type of user).

Kind regards, Ulf.

entry.title: Place of Supply should be a Mandatory field in "Create supplier invoice" fiori app for India GST
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are implementing S4HANA public cloud for Indian client  and we have a requirement to put a validation on the place of supply field in "Create supplier invoice" fiori app to make this field mandatory. 

Do we have a BADI in S4HANA public cloud to make this field mandatory ?

Thanks

Varun Arora

entry.title: Decimal separator as comma(,) for numbers in WebI4.3 report
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a cross table in Webi4.3 document BEx query as the source and I wanted to change the decimal separator from full stop(.) to comma (,) and did the below 3 settings but no luck. Please suggest if I need to perform any other?

  • a) Control panel -> region -> Additional settings-> Decimal symbol = , and save 
  • b) BI launch pad -> settings -> Application preferences -> Webi -> Select "Use my preferred viewing locale to format the data"
  • c) In BW system, the user's default settings are to get changed in BW system(su01) as shown below

entry.title: Re: Create a Profit & Loss Statement in SAP Analytics Cloud importing data from an Excel file
entry.content:
Hi Mariajose

I tried to open the excel file but it doesn't work properly. Can you please share the Excel?

Regards

Mani

entry.title: Re: Start page of SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition, the 4 pillars and documentation
entry.content:
Signavio analyzes and transform processes.
MP, like LeanIX, is more for IT and architecture, which Signavio is not.
From SAP Signavio, we now recommend LeanIX for the architecture.
Ultimately both topics (process transformation and enterprise transformaiton) are complementary. You can read a bit more about Signavio + LeanIX here.

entry.title: Introduction to MySelf
entry.content:
Hi All SAP Enthusiasts;
This is Sarosh Faheem, I'm a Certified Associate Consultant of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in a Consultancy Company, Associated With SAP as a "Golden Partner". specifically looking after the domain of Sourcing and Procurement and Sales as an Associate Consultant.
So I'm thrilled to Connect With you as Much as Much as you are. To Collaborate, Share and Grow Each Other in the World of SAP. I look  forward to connecting With You all

 #SAPBeginners, #Enthusiasts, #SAP_Consultant

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP Data Warehouse Cloud OData API with PowerBI via a Blank Query
entry.content:
Hi @gustavokath 

Thanks for reply,

I solved the problem as follows;

When I give the URL parameter as ‘https://your-dwc-tenant....sap/api/v1/dwc/catalog/’,

it automatically adds $metadata to the end. but when I went to ‘https://your-dwc-tenant....sap/api/v1/dwc/catalog/$metadata’ from the browser, I got a 404 error.

Then I went to ‘https://<tenant_url>/api/v1/dwc/catalog/assets’ from the browser and got an analytical model link from the returned json and gave it to the URL parameter in PowerBI. This way I reached the data.

entry.title: Re: Send files along with SAP Build Process Automation AP...
entry.content:
Hello @akhthaaralibadhusha ,

This is not possible to send any document with the API. You can however upload the document in any CMIS based document repository like SAP Document Management Service and add the file ID or the link of the uploaded document.

Regards,
Archana

entry.title: Re: SAP FE ListReport to filter list to show newly create...
entry.content:
Hi,

I don't quite get the user experience if the table simply shows the newly created record suddenly - why can you not have the standard behavior that shows also other records in addition to the newly created one?!

But if this is really required, I am thinking maybe the getSelectionVariant() and setSelectionVariant() of the filter bar building block should help. The filter bar building block is internally used by List Report and you should be able to reference it by ID in your extension code.

Best Regards,

   Sandeep

entry.content:

Does this issue still persists? Please confirm.

entry.title: Re: Spool request not generated for custom form in EWM
entry.content:
Hi,

If your spool data is well set up in SCWM/60000431, then check that your condition records parameters are well configured in /SCWM/PRPI_GCM. Put your custom form , printer and others mandatory data.

Best regards,

Kolawolé

entry.title: Re: What is dump DBIF_NTAB_TABLE_NOT_FOUND?
entry.content:
You access a table which is not/wrongly/inconsistently defined in dictionnary.

  • Call SE11 on the table and execute 'check' option of the menu.

Thanks,
Uldis

entry.title: Re: how to check entitiled/purchased products
entry.content: Please contact SAP Order Management per KBA 1660069.

entry.title: Re: What is dump DBIF_NTAB_TABLE_NOT_FOUND?
entry.content: Hi, thanks for the reply. Actually it's a name like "YY_XULAF27KQYQ". Is it a temp class generated for the interface?

entry.title: Re: Job Date Functions
entry.content: For my understanding, the job date function can be handled by both Server-Based and User-Based. The Server-Based refers to the datestamp based on the server’s timezone and the User-Based refers to the datestamp based on the user’s timezone maintained in the Maintain Business Users app.

entry.title: Re: Important Notice: Changes in SAP Analytics Cloud Classic Stories & Analytic Applications
entry.content:
Hello @Tunir 

Thank you for this Blog! Without that I would have not been aware of the MUST of a migration.

I would have assumed that SAP officially communicates this to all SAC Tenant Owners, and not only via a Blog 😞

However, what I would really like to complain about is still the missing feature equality of the classic mode and ODE. You don't mention in one single sentence here, until when this happens.

Yes there are some items on the official Roadmap, but obviously there is no final item, which claims the full feature availability. Only one small example: "Pin to Home" ... is still not available! I found this lately in Q1, 2025, but now I cannot see it anymore?!

When does SAP communicate officially, which items are on the internal roadmap of features to be closed, and which features will be ignored and not be impelemtend in ODE?

Thanks, Martin

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for HTML (WebGUI) SSO using Kerberos - Connec...
entry.content:
Hi Shaun,

 

I couldn't come up with any Kerberos scenario that would fit your description.

My guess would be that some other thing interferes here. Could you double check if your systems are configured to issue SAP Logon Tickets (parameter login/create_sso2_ticket)? You might run into a situation where the first system issues a Logon Ticket (browser cookie MYSAPSSO2). When you access the second system, this Ticket is sent there as well and the system tries to authenticate you based on that cookie. However, that doesn't work. And then the default setup in SICF is to only try one non-password authentication method and skips from the failed SAP LogonTicket  authentication directly to user/password. The setting in SICF is the checkmark "Use all logon procedures" on the Logon Data tab in a service.

Of course, there are many variables here, but this would be my first guess, that would explain your system behaviour.

 

Best regards,
Tobias

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for HTML (WebGUI) SSO using Kerberos - Connec...
entry.content:
I resolved the issue by changing the /sap/bc/gui/sap/its/webgui service to "Alternative Logon Procedure" and moving the "SPNEGO Authentication" to the top of the Procedure List.

And then I went searching for a note to see if "my" fix was documented anywhere ... and of course it was ...

2544749 - Single Sign On authentication doesn't work as expected for Browser access to Application Server ABAP - SAP for Me

Thanks SAP.  30 years of working in the Basis space (started in 1995 on 2.2e) and I'm still learning.

Cheers

Shaun

entry.title: Open Sourcing the SAP Cloud SDK for Java: Part 2 - Moving Code
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud SDK is a library for developing applications on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). While its version for JavaScript has been open source since 2020, the Java version has been open sourced only recently with the release of version 5.0.0.

This blog post is the second of a series where I go over the process of how we moved the SAP Cloud SDK for Java from an internal code base and development ecosystem to an open source project on GitHub.

Part one covered the non-technical planning and preparation that was necessary to open source the project. Finally, part three will cover the transformation of the CI/CD pipeline and the surrounding automations.

📈 The Overall Strategy

Right from the get-go it was clear that we would release a new major version of the SDK once the open source migration was complete. The open source version would come with a new license and significant changes to the feature scope, dropping support for some legacy features and platforms. With the current version being 4.X at the time, we set the goal to release version 5.0.0 as the first open source version of the SDK.

Still, we would have to continue developing the current version 4 for some time, at least up until a few weeks before the release of version 5. And even after that, we would have to maintain version 4 and provide bug fixes and security updates for some time.

Next, we had decided to replace our existing (Jenkins) pipelines and related tooling (e.g. for static code checks, dependency updates etc.) in favor of more modern alternatives that are more suitable for open source projects. We decided to completely switch to GitHub Actions for our pipelines and automations, and also use other tools provided by GitHub for code quality and security checks (e.g. CodeQL, Dependabot). This would require some time to set up and test.

Finally, we wanted to have a smooth transition with as little downtime to the development process as possible. Ideally, we copy over the code to the new repository and have all the CI/CD tooling and automations in place and working, guaranteeing the same or even better code quality compared to the internal repository.

🚚 How We Moved the Code Base

With these requirements in mind we came up with a plan to move the code base over to the open source repository. It comprised of three steps:

  1. Copy a representative sample of the code to the open source repository and develop the CI/CD pipelines.
  2. Create a v5 branch in the internal repository and develop the open source version in parallel.
  3. Move the v5 branch over to the open source repository and press the release button.

Copying a sample allowed us to develop the CI/CD pipeline and all related tooling in parallel to the code development. Also, it allowed us to split the code base as late as possible, reducing the time period where we need to maintain two repositories.

🧪 Creating a Representative Sample

Once we had created the empty repository on GitHub we copied around 5-10 modules or roughly 10 % of the code base over to the open source repository. We carefully chose a subset of the code that we knew would change relatively little in the near future and that would be representative of the whole code base. The code sample was representative in the sense that it included various different kinds of Maven modules: Typical modules that would get shipped as JAR, parent and BOM modules that are used for dependency management, modules that would get shipped as a Maven plugin or Maven archetype and test modules that wouldn't get shipped.

This allowed us to implement our CI/CD pipelines on a subset of the code that would be representative of the whole code base. Also, this meant this work can be done in parallel and completely independent of the development on the internal repository. In the end, the sample code allowed us to fully prepare all CI/CD tooling with a high degree of confidence that once we move over the rest of the code base everything would work as expected.

🔀 Creating a Branch for V5

We then created a v5 branch in the internal repository and started removing any code that would not be open sourced. We also set up an automation right away that would merge any changes done to v4 automatically into the v5 branch.

That way we could continue developing the current version 4 without the risk of forgetting to port relevant changes to the v5 branch. Still, maintaining two branches is additional work, so we pushed creating the v5 branch back as far as possible.

↗️ Moving to the Open Source Repository

Finally, once all changes required for the open source migration on the v5 branch were complete and the open source repository was set up with all required CI/CD tooling, we moved over the v5 branch to the open source repository. This step almost fully replaced the existing code sample we had copied over earlier. Only the parts relevant to the CI/CD setup were kept and merged with the incoming v5 branch.

On the 2nd of October around 2500 files were copied over from the internal to the open source repository with this pull request. We implemented the necessary adjustments for the CI/CD pipelines to work on the full code base (e.g. adjusting the thresholds for the code scans and test coverage, adjusting the copied over pom.xml files etc.).

Copying over also meant we would lose the commit history of the code. While technically we could have kept it, it would have contained all the internal code and information we had to remove from the code base. Therefore, we decided to start with a clean commit history in the open source repository.

The process finished on the 4th of October where the pull request was merged and the internal v5 branch archived.

📖  Summing Up

Overall, we were quite happy with how the move of the code base went. Having the work on pipelines and tooling done independently and in parallel to the development on the internal repository meant we didn't lose any time or sacrifice code quality in the process. It also made it easier to involve other from colleagues outside our development team who had more experience with GitHub Actions and helped us set up the pipelines.

Here is a timeline of the overall process:

In the third and final part of this series I will cover how we used GitHub Actions to implement our all our CI/CD pipelines and how we automated the release process.

entry.title: Re: SAP VCH AFL Installation
entry.content: I would recommend to open an incident to the component LO-VCH-ENG-AF and request the correct file to download.

entry.title: Re: cloud-cap-samples-java not starting locally
entry.content:
This error can occur when the automatic Maven build triggered by VS Code interferes with the Maven build of the application triggered by the CLI command.

The best way to circumvent this when using VS Code is to launch the application via VS Code natively, by opening the Application class and clicking the Run or Debug overlay.

Alternatively disable the VS Code auto build for Java, before launching Maven commands on the CLI.

entry.title: Re: What is dump DBIF_NTAB_TABLE_NOT_FOUND?
entry.content: Starting with Y and Z looks as some local object for your system, cannot help

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Nice information

entry.title: Re: what are the hana cloud services provided by sap till 2017?
entry.content: As I know, it should be the SAP HANA Enterprise Cloud and SAP HANA Cloud Platform

entry.title: Re: HANA Wrong File
entry.content: Please post your question correctly, so we can further try to help you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Customer Checkout - Billing localization for Port...
entry.content: @CJoubert

entry.title: Re: HANA instance access within steampunk instance for "S...
entry.content:
As I know, SAP does not provide direct access to the underlying HANA instance in a Steampunk environment.
I would recommend to open a feature request.

entry.title: Re: SAP Customer Checkout - Billing localization for Port...
entry.content: @CJoubert SAP Customer Checkout has the following approach. We have a list of countries for which we are officially localized from SAP side. Example Germany, France, Swiss, Austria, UK, Ireland. For these countries SAP does the localization and continuously adapt to changing regulations. For other countries we use our partner network. When a partner see there is value in localizing for a particular country then the partner does that. And our partners have localized not only for "portugal" but many many countries. We now have countries in 55+ countries of the world because of our extensive partner network. Hope it helps. If your question in answered correctly, kindly select the answer as "Right Answer". This helps others to follow and easily locate answers. Best Regards, Bikash Bansal

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for HTML (WebGUI) SSO using Kerberos - Connec...
entry.content: Exactly, I had the same thought when I read that. It's probably because of the logon tickets, and enabling the 'Use all logon procedures' checkbox should help. Even better, disable the issuance/acceptance of MYSAPSSO2 cookies altogether 😉

entry.title: Re: standard LIKP table not getting activated after append structure and doing se14
entry.content: 5 hours for tiny 25000 lines is really too much. Are you sure you are counting the total number of lines of LIKP in all clients of the system? Also, if you run it in dialog, there should be a timeout after some time, are you sure you didn't lose the connection with the backend and the adjustment is done?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:

This is available since Q2, QRC release of SAC

Thanks

Maheshwar

entry.title: Re: How to Block AR Invoice Based on Due Date and Counter...
entry.content:
Hello,

you can control this by creating a instead-of-trigger on the tables that the invoices are inserted into.

CREATE TRIGGER (Transact-SQL) - SQL Server | Microsoft Learn

Using INSTEAD OF triggers in SQL Server for DML operations (mssqltips.com)

Best regards

Clas

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Aylwin,

In SAC Enterprise we have a public model. the same model can be used in multiple stories. In People analytics stories we use a embedded model.

It is recommended to recreate them optimally again.

Thanks, Maheshwar

entry.title: Re: RF button modification
entry.content: That is right from the SAP RF environment point of view. You can't just hide the button in program. You should manage it via status.

entry.title: Re: How to enable Pay Statement service to view payslip in Employee Central System
entry.content:
Hello Magesh ,

Great article. I have a quick question please , is it possible to add an additional layer of password protection when someone opens the payslip ? This is a use case we have for operational staff who login to the same device in a shared work location and potentially forgot to sign off from the account. 

Thank you .

Saima

entry.title: Re: cloud-cap-samples-java not starting locally
entry.content: Hi Marc, Thanks a lot for your advice. May I just suggest that you add a corresponding remark in the readme file of the github project https://github.com/sap-samples/cloud-cap-samples-java ?

entry.title: Performance Runtime Crystal Reports 2020 for Visual Studio
entry.content:
Good morning.
I'm David Borsoi and I work at Tesy Software srl
We develop management software. In our software we use Crystal Reports runtime for previewing and printing reports.
Our software is developed in Delphi.
The software uses the Crystal Reports XI runtimes. This is the latest version which has the VCL components for communication with Delphi.
Now, we have opened a new project to update the version of Crystal Reports.
In the new project we have:
- created an object in Visual Studio, recalled by the management software which passes the information to Crystal Report.
- We use SAP Crystal Reports for Visual Studio (SP29) runtime (64-bit)

Printing and preview performance have become much worse: loading and printing the same report takes 3 times as long as before.
The report is the same. Data extraction times from SQL Server are the same.
Layout is very slow.

From Crystal Designer, previewing and printing are fast.
The problem appears to be runtime related.
I tried updating the runtimes to the latest SP35 but the result doesn't change.
Is this a known problem? Some idea?
Thank you

entry.title: Posting Material document via SAP Build
entry.content:
Hello fellow SAP-Builders,

I am currently trying to create an app that enables the user to perform certain goods movements (stock transfert from storage location to another storage location) by posting material docments in SAP Build. 
For this reason I tried to use API A_MaterialDocumentHeader (with nested entity to_MaterialDocumentItem), but for me it is not clear how to perform the creation of data for the nested entity of A_MaterialDocumentHeader in SAP Build. 

Can someone help here? 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG integration with CDQ
entry.content:
Assuming that you are on the SAP S/4HANA 2023 release. Please note that for the integration with CDQ (to get reference data), you must run the cloud-ready mode. For details, see the SAP documentation about configuration of data provider integration.

See also my blog about SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023.

entry.title: Re: Production kit
entry.content:
@DominikTylczyn Actually this is my code:

 

  METHOD if_ex_md_modify_prodvers~modify_production_version.
    " ins - 29.07.2022 - cvivo: select production version for set BOMs depending on MRP situation

    " The BAdI will explore all the co-products in the bills of materials for the current planned order and
    " calculate the available stock for each. If this available stock is negative, it will be stored in an internal table.
    "The alternative with the lowest sum of negative quantities will be picked.

    "We do not explore substitutions individually because if alternative 1 has component A (-5 stock) and component B (-15 stock),
    " whereas alternative 2 has component A (-5 stock) and component B (3 stock); at total calculation, A is summed in both alternatives,
    " leading to its compensation, and only substitute components affect to total differences among alternatives.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_version,
             verid TYPE mkal-verid,
             stlal TYPE mkal-stlal,
             stlan TYPE mkal-stlan,
             menge TYPE menge_d,
           END OF ty_version.

    DATA: t_stb_aux        TYPE TABLE OF stpox,
          t_coproducts     TYPE TABLE OF stpox,
          t_coproducts_aux TYPE TABLE OF stpox,
          t_position       TYPE TABLE OF stpox,
          t_mrp            TYPE TABLE OF bapi_mrp_ind_lines,
          selected         TYPE mkal,
          t_version        TYPE TABLE OF ty_version.


    SELECT SINGLE  FROM tvarvc
      WHERE name EQ 'ZPP_PV_SETS'
        AND low EQ _matnr
        AND high EQ _werks
        INTO (result).

    IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
      SELECT * FROM mkal
        WHERE matnr EQ _matnr
          AND werks EQ _werks
          AND bdatu GE -datlo
          AND adatu LE -datlo
          AND prfg_r EQ '1'  " valid routing
          AND prfg_s EQ '1' " valid BOM
          AND mksp NE '1' " not locked
        INTO TABLE (t_mkal).

      IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
        SORT t_mkal BY stlan stlal. DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM t_mkal COMPARING stlan stlal. " to calculate totals by STLAL we need to ensure there are no duplicate values

        LOOP AT t_mkal INTO DATA(mkal).
          CALL FUNCTION 'CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2'
            EXPORTING
              capid                 = 'PP01'
              emeng                 = 1
              datuv                 = sy-datlo
              mtnrv                 = im_matnr
              werks                 = im_werks
              stlan                 = mkal-stlan
              stlal                 = mkal-stlal
              verid                 = mkal-verid
            TABLES
              stb                   = t_stb_aux
            EXCEPTIONS
              alt_not_found         = 1
              call_invalid          = 2
              material_not_found    = 3
              missing_authorization = 4
              no_bom_found          = 5
              no_plant_data         = 6
              no_suitable_bom_found = 7
              conversion_error      = 8
              OTHERS                = 9.

          DELETE t_stb_aux WHERE kzkup IS INITIAL.

          CHECK t_stb_aux[] IS NOT INITIAL.

          APPEND INITIAL LINE TO t_version ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<version>).
          MOVE-CORRESPONDING mkal TO <version>.

          LOOP AT t_stb_aux ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<stb>).
            <stb>-mnglg = 0. " we will use this field for MRP available quantity result

            CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_MATERIAL_STOCK_REQ_LIST'
              EXPORTING
                material      = <stb>-idnrk
                plant         = <stb>-werks
              TABLES
                mrp_ind_lines = t_mrp.

            DELETE t_mrp WHERE avail_date GT im_date.

            CHECK t_mrp[] IS NOT INITIAL.

            DATA(index) = lines( t_mrp ).
            DATA(mrp) = t_mrp[ index ]. " last entry for the date will be the one with the actual available quantity

            <stb>-mnglg =  mrp-avail_qty1.

            IF <stb>-mnglg LT 0. " positive values will be discarded for version selection
              ADD <stb>-mnglg TO <version>-menge.
              APPEND <stb> TO t_coproducts. " just for informative purpose
            ENDIF.
          ENDLOOP.
        ENDLOOP.

        CHECK t_version[] IS NOT INITIAL.

        SORT t_version BY menge ASCENDING.
        DATA(version) = t_version[ 1 ]. " selected version will be the one with the lowest available quantity (considering just shortages)

        ch_verid = version-verid.
        ch_changed = 'X'.
      ELSE.
        EXIT.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Fix to failed import of production documents
entry.content:
Hi all,

I was entrusted with the task of setting up SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and connecting it to an S/4HANA on Prem (2023). I followed the instructions in the documentation.

Connecting to SAP S/4HANA | SAP Help Portal

After working through the steps, I encountered the following problem. The production documents and the cost centers could not be imported due to an unknown error. Other entities, such as products, product groups, ... could be imported completely and error-free. As a result, the CO2 footprint calculation could not be continued. A ticket at SAP provided me with the decisive clue.
Importing the following SAP Notes into S4 was able to fix the error.

  • 3342592
  • 3363554 

Have fun with the calculation of the CO2-footprints!

SAP Sustainability Footprint Management

entry.title: Build Process Automation and Work Zone Connection
entry.content:
Hello experts,

we have been using SAP Build Process Automation  for a year to automate our processes. In the future we want to use the workflow capability of BPA. That's the reason why we want to connect Build Process Automation with Build Work Zone, standard edition. Is it necessary that both applications are located in the same subaccount in the BTP or can they have different subaccounts?

Kind regards,

Theresa

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation- Right way to process a...
entry.content:
Hello @rds0871 ,

As of now, you can use Automation Activity and loop over all the regions and extract the approvers. Then you can send an email (Outlook option in Automation ) to send email. Going forward, once the loop concept is directly available in the process then you can apply the same logic in the process itself (i.e. using loop and then send email using Email activity)

Regards,
Archana

entry.title: SAP IBP Formula similar to excel CEILING function
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have been searching for answers on this one, but couldn't find any. Perhaps i will strike some luck here.

I need to calculate a CEILING between two different inputs, for example a KF and an integer attribute:

KF_A: 100  

Attribute_B: 15 -> The multiple to which KF_A will be rounded

-> The formula should result in 105 which is the KF_A rounded up to the nearest multiple of Attribute B

There is CEIL IBP formula, but it only allows for one input, therefore not usable in this case..

There is an MS Excel formula that works perfectly: https://support.microsoft.com/en-au/Key Figure Formulaoffice/ceiling-function-0a5cd7c8-0720-4f0a-bd2c-c943e510899f 

 

Any help will be greatly appreciated.

Thanks! Andrei

entry.title: SAP BW Bridge InfoObject Hierarchy Re-Use in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Community

I have a question regarding creating a dimension (with a hierarchy) using entities that are imported from SAP BW Bridge.

I have shell converted the info objects from BW system into SAP BW Bridge. I then imported the entities into Datasphere. I would like to now create the dimension in Datasphere.

I followed some of the content on how to create the dimension but seem to always be missing some piece of information. I used the document below and followed some links attached to the document, but I experienced some errors and warnings.
Creating a Dimension | SAP Help Portal

My questions are:
Do I need to create a dimension (semantic usage) type for each entity (attribute, text and hierarchy entities) or do I have to create a dimension using the attribute entity with an association to the text entity. And do something similar for the hierarchy entity?

I am struggling to find content explaining this. May you please point me in the right direction in attaining documentation that explains how to stitch the info object by using these different entities in Datasphere?

Thank you:

Kind regards

Cornelius Faurie (obo Suli Sujee)

Thanks

Abdul

entry.title: Related to Data migration, standard conversion program.

Dear All,

"How can I ensure the standard programs (NSDM_mig_Material_doc, NSDM_mig_Fill_extract, Nsdm_mIg_create_perfy) are running properly to migrate ECC tables (such as MKPF, MSEG, Mara, Marc, etc's) into the S/4 Hana tables MATDOC and MATDOC_Extract for direct update ?.

I am new to data migrations and conversions. I request experts to suggest steps, references, or any helpful guidance on how to proceed. Please help me."

Regards.

Harsha.

entry.title: Re: Mastering SAP S/4 HANA Cloud: Your Guide to Central Business Configuration
entry.content: Nice blog.

entry.title: Re: Capturing the approval form status on SAP BPA (Build...
entry.content:
Hello @Isyraf_Afifi , 

If you want to check the status of the User Task outside the process, then you can use Task APIs - with which you can get the task status as READY, RESERVED, CANCELED, COMPLETED based on the respective task instance ID.

If you also want to preserve the outcome of the task like Approved or Rejected then you have to get it added in the Process Context like you can have a explicit option Button say "Approve" or "Reject" and user has to explicitly select that besides selecting the button OR if you have SAPUI5 based user task UI then on button click you can call the respective Task API to added the outcome in the process. These are the only two options available as-of-now. In future, the form button outcomes will be available as process context. 

Hope that helps,
Archana

entry.title: Focused Insight relevant capabilities in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi,
 
I am looking for a similar capability as Focused Insight Solman to be in SAP Cloud ALM. Could you guide me how to setup this?
Custom dashboard containing system related information.
 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Focused Insight relevant capabilities in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
At this time, SAP Cloud ALM does not provide functionality to build custom dashboards that incorporate data from the various use-cases in our product (e.g. Health Monitoring, Integration & Exception Monitoring, etc.).
There are 2 options regarding what your next steps are.

  1. You can access the SAP Customer Influence campaign relevant to SAP Cloud ALM for Operations and check for existing requests matching your desire. If one exists, you can vote on it to show your support and indicate to our Developers that it is a feature you want. If no entry exists, you can create one to outline your business need. See this KBA for more information: 3391676 - SAP Continuous Influence for SAP Cloud ALM
  2. You can use the existing SAP Cloud ALM APIs to pull data from SAP Cloud ALM, and incorporate that in an external Custom dashboard or business insights software. For example, SAP Analytics Cloud can be used to pull data SAP Cloud ALM for Operations use case data via our APIs and display it in this interface. More information about the APIs is available at these links:
    • SAP Business Accelerator Hub, SAP Cloud ALM REST APIs
    • API guide for SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: Supplier Portal

entry.title: Re: Cause of HANA Cloud High CPU
entry.content:
High CPU usage in HANA Cloud can be cause by too many processes. So, check workload using SAP HANA Cockpit or Studio. Look for long SQL statements, high memory usage, or many active connections.
It can be also caused by Delta merge operations, because this process moves data and can be CPU-intensive. Check frequency and duration in the SAP HANA Cockpit. If too frequent, it might be due to high data change rates or small delta memory limits.
Also, a poorly optimized SQL queries can consume a lot of CPU. Use the SQL Plan Cache in the SAP HANA Cockpit to identify inefficient queries. Look for queries with high execution times, high CPU usage, or high read/write operations.
Then, you should check insufficient hardware resources for specifications and compare them with the recommended specifications for SAP HANA.
Bugs in the SAP HANA software can cause high CPU usage. Check the SAP Notes and SAP Community for any known issues.

entry.title: Re: Querying intermediary node in Graphical Calculation V...
entry.content: You cannot directly query an intermediary node in a graphical calculation view in SAP HANA. May you have to check a workaround.

entry.title: Re: Dump on HR_EVENT_UPDATE - DBSQL Duplicate key
entry.content:
Hi @raymind_giuseppi, 

I found the problem. When I first run the program, the server crushed. And somehow the data was being treated as not processed. Thus, prompting the duplicated key error while processing new data.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Hiding Workflow Step Names in Business Process Automa...
entry.content:
Hello @AlessandroB3 , 

Hiding the step name is not possible in MyInbox. You can customize fields in Inbox using these link (link1 | link2)

entry.title: Re: Searching RFC to read Equipment system status
entry.content:
It sounds like you're looking for a Remote Function Call (RFC) module to retrieve the system status of an equipment, similar to how you might use the STATUS_READ function module. RFC allows you to call functions in a remote system, typically in SAP environments.

There isn't a specific RFC module named "STATUS_READ" as such in the standard SAP library. However, you can achieve the functionality you're looking for by developing your own RFC-enabled function module or using existing SAP standard function modules that provide the required system status information.

To create your own RFC-enabled function module:

1. Identify the system status information you need to retrieve.
2. Write ABAP code to gather this information.
3. Make sure your function module is RFC-enabled.
4. Register your function module as an RFC destination in your SAP system.

Alternatively, you can search for existing SAP standard function modules that provide the system status information you need. You can use transaction code SE37 to search for function modules based on specific criteria or explore SAP documentation for available function modules related to system status.

entry.title: UK Payroll - Preparing for the end of P11Ds - Understanding SAP’s Payrolling of Benefits Solution
entry.content:
In partnership with the UKISUG SAP will be presenting two webinars to introduce the topic of payrolling benefits and explaining how SAP Payroll (PY-GB) handles this.

Sessions are open to all and we encourage business users as well as IT support and consultants to join.

Part 1 - Wednesday 17th July 11.00 - 12.00 (GMT)
This session will introduce the basic concepts of the payrolling of benefits processing in SAP.
Registration link: https://www.sapusers.org/events/4239/booking/guest

Part 2 - Tuesday 17th September 13.30 - 14.30 (GMT) 
This session will build on the first going in to more complex scenarios so please ensure you have attended the July session (or watched back the recording) before attending the part 2 webinar.
Registration link: https://www.sapusers.org/events/4261/booking/guest

Speakers:
Natasha  Robinson, Local Product Manager, SAP
Elaine Miller, Localization Product Manager, SAP

Please check if the functionality is still available on trial accounts or not.

Regards,

Rakesh

entry.title: Re: Error with using table function in query
entry.content: The error message is indicating that you cannot use table or field aliases as inputs to a table function in HANA DB. In your case, you are trying to use the aliases A and B as inputs to the table function "Function", which is not allowed in HANA DB. To solve this issue, you can try to use the actual table or field names instead of the aliases.
